transcription.text: Why should I bother with life insurance? I'm single. That's what orchard owner Walter Horst said some years back to the man from money. I was putting away any spare cash toward a nest egg. But the money man got Walter thinking. While Walter worked towards having his own orchard, he could build a nest egg through insurance. Besides, the money man said, premiums are lower while you're young. And when you do get married, you'll have a head start on real family protection. Well, I got married, all right. Now I've got a wife and five kids and my own orchard too. Looking back, I'm glad I started that insurance program when I did. I rely on that money man's advice more and more.

metadata.title: Mony Mutual Life Insurance company of New York
